ground_truth.title: what i pay kagi for
ground_truth.author: suliman
ground_truth.main_content: In this post, I will talk about my experience using Kagi for my fourth month now, how I justified its price for myself, and my configuration of the service. Kagi offers personalized search results that are manually configured. By default, the search results are as trash as those of other search engines, but you can block, 'lower' or 'raise' domains and TLDs yourself. You can even rank YouTube channels or block them entirely. In total, I have 61 items ranked manually, including a block on the 'ai' TLD. You can create custom lenses that prioritize sites based on rules you set. I have one for government sites that only surfaces results with my government's TLD and another that searches official documentation only. Kagi lets you create your own bangs that would probably not be added to the general DDG library for lack of their popularity. You can modify URLs in your search results with Regex redirect rules. Kagi shipped with a translation, proofreading and dictionary service within the same subscription which I hadn't known about since they don't advertise them on their website. If I can get all those search features along with a unified dictionary, translation and proofreader interface, it's kind of worth it.
